title: Git Mental Model, Setup, Help, and Repository Safety · Spiral Review
summary: Adopt an unfamiliar repository without overwriting identity, hooks, ignored files, or team policy. Learners retrieve rules and procedures before seeing feedback. Planned artifact: Extend the evidence record for a repository orientation and configuration evidence notebook after a delayed changed case. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Explain Git history as content-addressed snapshots connected by parent links and named references.
Distinguish working tree, Git directory, common directory, bare repository, linked worktree, and nested repository boundaries.
Inspect system, global, local, worktree, command, and environment configuration with origin and scope evidence.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.shell:
# git-basics cumulative repository evidence notebook
# Git operations change only disposable simulated repository state.
# Inspect status, diffs, graph, refs, and recovery before every mutation.

steps.title: Retrieve the complete evidence cycle
steps.instruction: Order the Snapshots Graph evidence cycle for this review task before implementing it.
steps.why: Correct order keeps Snapshots Graph assumptions visible before execution and verifies its result against a boundary case afterward.

content.text: Repository /home/learner/lab uses branch main, local author scope, and Git 4.x. Identify working tree, Git directory, config origins, installed help, and recovery reference before adopting it. Competency probe: Explain Git history as content-addressed snapshots connected by parent links and named references.

content.title: Ordering constraint
content.text: Order work so Snapshots Graph has a declared contract before execution and a boundary or counterexample after it.

hints: Name the Snapshots Graph precondition or invariant that the review case must preserve.

Trace this concrete case before choosing: Repository /home/learner/lab uses branch main, local author scope, and Git 4.x. Identify working tree, Git directory, config origins, installed help, and recovery reference before adopting it. Competency probe: Explain Git history as content-addressed snapshots connected by parent links and named references.
Use the documented misconception as a counterexample target: Git stores only line-by-line differences between every adjacent version.

options.text: Inspect repository boundary, config origin, current operation, worktree, index, refs, and graph for the review Snapshots Graph case
Preserve uncommitted work and a recovery ref, then state intended tree and topology for the review Snapshots Graph case
Preview and perform the least destructive simulated Git operation for the review Snapshots Graph case
Verify status, staged and range diffs, graph, tests, remote target, and recovery path for the review Snapshots Graph case

steps.title: Record what required effort
steps.instruction: Write the review evidence note for Help Version Safety: explain the governing condition, one failure, and how this concrete case was verified — Repository /home/learner/lab uses branch main, local author scope, and Git 6.x. Identify working tree, Git directory, config origins, installed help, and recovery reference before adopting it. Competency probe: Use installed help, version evidence, dry runs, status, and backups before following repository-changing instructions.
steps.why: Retrieving Help Version Safety through the concrete a repository orientation and configuration evidence notebook case exposes causal gaps that recognition alone can hide.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Use installed help, version evidence, dry runs, status, and backups before following repository-changing instructions.

checks.description: The evidence cycle runs from problem framing through changed-case validation.
checks.failureMessage: Frame the decision before modeling, and validate after obtaining a result.
